mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 8 Series engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned BMW 8 Series engine typically costs £3,800–£12,000+ supply only [BMW-QUOTE] — from the B58 in the 840i at £3,800–£6,000 to the N63 in the M850i at £7,000–£12,000+. The S63 in the M8 costs £9,000–£14,000+. Fitting adds £650–£2,500. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 8 Series engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58B30 straight-six in the 840i — the most dependable modern BMW engine in our dataset. The M70 and M73 V12s in the E31 are also surprisingly durable — 200k+ miles is possible with proper maintenance. Avoid the N63 V8 unless you have a warranty.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 8 Series engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine outright — but the N63B44 V8 (M850i) without a documented timing chain guide replacement, valve stem seal history, and comprehensive warranty is the one purchase we consistently advise against. The N63's hot-vee design creates fundamental weaknesses that BMW has never fully resolved. A B58 840i has none of these caveats.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my 8 Series engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: By engine: rattling on cold start (N63 timing chain guides), blue smoke on startup and excessive oil consumption (N63 valve stem seals), oil in coolant (N63 turbo failure), coolant loss (B58 water pump/expansion tank). On the E31, electrical gremlins and cooling system failure are the main causes of engine death. Caught early, it's a repair; ignored, it's a £3,800–£12,000+ engine [BMW-QUOTE]. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 8 Series engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends entirely on generation and engine. Against a £70,000 M8, a £9,000–£14,000+ S63 recon is 13–20% — ✅ safe. Against a £35,000 used M850i, a £7,000–£12,000+ N63 recon is 20–34% — ⚠️ watch. Against an E31 V12, engine work is restoration — the values justify the investment. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. Use our calculator for your car.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 8 Series should I buy?

acceptedAnswer.text: G15 840i with the B58 — the most dependable engine in the range, 340 bhp, 33 mpg, and the grand tourer experience without the V8 bills. For diesel, G15 840d with B57 — 40+ mpg and bulletproof. For the full experience, G15 M850i with N63 — but budget £7,000–£12,000+ for a recon and buy the best warranty. For the collector, E31 850i with the M70 V12 — rising classic, appreciating asset. Avoid used M850i without a comprehensive warranty.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 8 Series engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and in the vehicle documentation; block stamping is visible from below on most engines. Or tell us your generation, body style, model year and variant (840i, 840d, M850i, M8) and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted specialists will confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: BMW 8 Series vs 6 Series — which is more reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Mechanically they share engines across the same eras — the B58, N63, and B57 story is similar. The 8 Series is the flagship GT; the 6 Series was the previous generation. The B58 in the 840i is more reliable than the N63 in the M850i — buy the straight-six. The 8 Series offers more presence and prestige; the 6 Series (F06/F12/F13) offers more value.

hero.h1: 8 Series Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every BMW 8 Series engine across two generations and three body styles — from the M70 V12 of the original E31 to the B58 straight-six and N63 V8 of the modern G15. Real UK replacement costs, known failure patterns, and honest ownership economics for BMW's grand tourer flagship. Backed by 150+ real UK 8 Series engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

metrics.ourCall: ⚠️ The 8 Series rule — grand tourer, grand bills: an N63 recon at £7,000–£12,000 against a £30,000 used G15 850i is 23–40% of the car's value — ⚠️ watch. Against a £100,000 G16, the same repair is just 7–12% — ✅ safe. The E31 is now a classic where engine work is restoration, not repair.
★★★☆☆ — the B58 straight-six is the modern gold standard; the N63 V8 carries the hot-vee caveats; the E31's V8s and V12s are ageing but fundamentally robust.
B58B30 (840i) — the most dependable modern BMW engine — and the M70/M73 V12 in the E31, which are surprisingly durable
N63B44 (M850i) — timing chain guides, valve stem seals, turbo failures, excessive oil consumption
G15 840i with B58 — the straight-six is bulletproof, the chassis is magnificent, and the V8 caveats don't apply
G15 M850i with N63 — 523 bhp of twin-turbo V8, but budget £7,000–£12,000 for a recon and buy the best warranty
N63 oil consumption / timing chain guides — shared-platform data [BMW-VERIFIED]
👑 The Flagship GT — BMW's most expensive non-M model and the car with the highest parts bills in the range

ownershipVerdict.oneLineVerdict: The 8 Series is BMW's grand tourer flagship — beautiful, powerful, and expensive to keep. The B58 840i is the sensible choice; the N63 M850i is the emotional one. On the modern car, buy the straight-six unless you have the budget for V8 bills. On the E31, buy the V12 and treat it as a restoration project.

rows.value: N63B44 (M850i) — timing chain guides, valve stem seals, turbo failure
H2: BMW 8 Series Generations — Find Your Vehicle

rows.metric: Sub-headline: Two generations, thirty years apart, and one grand touring mission. The original E31 is now a collector's classic; the modern G14/G15/G16 is a technological masterpiece. Choose your era — and your budget.

rows.value: Our Verdict: The original 8 Series — a pillarless coupe with pop-up headlights and a V12 that defined 1990s BMW. The M70 and M73 V12s are surprisingly durable if maintained; the V8s (M60/M62) carry the same Nikasil liner caveats as the E34 5 Series. Now a rising classic where engine work is restoration, not repair. The 850CSi with the S70 V12 is a six-figure collector's item.

rows.metric: BMW 8 Series Coupé G15 🔥 Most Enquired — 150+ enquiries (2025) [BMW-VERIFIED]

rows.value: Our Verdict: The return of the 8 Series after 19 years — and the B58 straight-six in the 840i is the most dependable engine ever fitted to the badge. The N63 V8 in the M850i carries the hot-vee caveats; the M8 with the S63 is the full-fat experience. The 840i is the sensible choice; the M850i is the emotional one. Buy the B58 unless you have V8 money.

rows.metric: Our Verdict: The open-top 8 Series — same engines as the G15, but with a fabric roof that adds weight and complexity. The B58 in the 840i Convertible is the sensible pick; the M850i Convertible is the weekend toy. Check the roof mechanism on any used example.

rows.value: Our Verdict: The four-door 8 Series — the most practical 8 Series and the one with the most presence. Same engines as the G15/G14, but the G16's added length and rear doors make it a genuine four-seater. The 840i Gran Coupé is the sweet spot; the M850i is the statement.
The Engine Choice — The Line That Matters Most
Read the full comparison: BMW 8 Series 840i vs M850i →

Correction applied (internal): The "840i" badge uses the B58B30 straight-six petrol (3.0L, 333–340 bhp). The "840d" uses the B57D30 straight-six diesel (3.0L, 315–340 bhp). Both are excellent engines, but the B58 petrol is the more refined and reliable of the two. The "M850i" uses the N63B44 twin-turbo V8 (4.4L, 523 bhp) — a completely different engine family with different reliability characteristics. The "M8" uses the S63B44 — a high-performance version of the N63.

engineDatabase.h2: BMW 8 Series Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine ever fitted to a BMW 8 Series — verified codes, specifications and real UK ownership data.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: B58B30 and N63B44 ratings: 90% confidence — high-volume modern engines with deep specialist rebuild history and clear failure patterns from BMW TSB data and 100+ specialist interviews. B57D30 and S63B44 ratings: 85% confidence — well-documented patterns from the G30/G11 eras. E31 engines: Expert-knowledge tier — decades of documented patterns and enthusiast records, not proprietary volume data. Data note (internal) — corrections applied: Correction applied: The source data lists "8 Series" as a single entry, but the 8 Series is two distinct generations. The modern G14/G15/G16 (2018–2026) shares engines with the 5 Series (G30) and 7 Series (G11/G70). The original E31 (1990–1999) is not captured in the proprietary data. Correction applied: The N63B44 in the M850i carries the same hot-vee V8 caveats as the F01 750i and G30 M550i — timing chain guides made of brittle plastic, valve stem seal wear, excessive oil consumption, turbo failures from heat soak. BMW's later N63TU updates address some issues but the fundamental hot-vee design remains a weakness. Correction applied: The M850i N63 produces 523 bhp — significantly more than the F01 750i's 407–450 bhp. The extra power means more heat, more stress, and potentially more failures. Correction applied: The E31's M70 V12 (850i) and M73 V12 (850Ci) are surprisingly durable engines — the M70 was BMW's first production V12 and is known for high mileage if maintained. The S70 in the 850CSi is a rare and valuable engine — only a handful were produced.

commonProblems.subHeadline: The six most common failure patterns across the 8 Series range — symptoms, costs and urgency.

problems.description: The defining modern 8 Series issue — plastic timing chain guides break apart, sending metal fragments through the engine. Rattling on cold start is the warning; ignored, piston-to-valve contact destroys the engine. Affects G14/G15/G16 M850i. £7,000–£12,000+ recon.

problems.issue: N63 Valve Stem Seal & Oil Consumption
problems.description: The N63's companion failure — worn valve stem seals cause blue smoke on startup and excessive oil consumption (1L per 1,000 miles). Affects M850i. Often requires engine-out repair.
The N63's hot-vee design causes turbocharger failure from excessive heat and poor oil return. High-pressure fuel pumps can fail, dumping raw fuel into cylinders. Affects M850i.
The B58's known weak point — coolant expansion tank leaks and water pump failures. Affects G14/G15/G16 840i (2018+). £400–£800 repair, not catastrophic if caught early.
The B57 diesel's primary weak points — DPF clogging on urban-driven cars and injector issues. Affects G14/G15/G16 840d (2018+). Well-documented, repairable.
The original 8 Series' common non-engine issues — complex electronics (EDC, ABS, airbag modules) and cooling system plastic components degrade. Check everything on any E31.

marketIntelligence.h2: BMW 8 Series Market Intelligence — What 150+ UK Owners Told Us in 2025

signals.demandTrend: ⬆ Rising — used 840i cars entering affordable range [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]
➡ Stable — collector market, not failure-driven [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]
➡ Stable — the N63's reputation is well-known [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]

insights: Interesting insight: The 8 Series generates fewer enquiries than any other BMW model in our dataset — and that's a good thing. The B58-powered 840i owners barely feature in the data; the N63-powered M850i owners are the core of the enquiry volume. The B58 is proving as reliable in the flagship GT as it is in the 3 Series. (Editorial synthesis — specialist sense-check before publishing.)

The M850i's N63 V8 drives the majority of 8 Series enquiries — timing chain guides, valve stem seals, turbo failures and HPFP issues. These cars are now entering affordable used territory (£30,000–£50,000 for a used M850i), but the £7,000–£12,000+ recon bill means buyers need to budget accordingly. The 840i is the sensible choice; the M850i is the emotional one. (Editorial synthesis.)

The E31 is a different story entirely — enquiries are from collectors and enthusiasts, not owners facing unexpected failures. The V12s are appreciating assets; the V8s are rising classics. Engine work on an E31 is restoration, not repair. (Editorial synthesis.)

editorialPullQuote.title: The One Thing We'd Tell Every BMW 8 Series Buyer
editorialPullQuote.quote: If you buy one 8 Series based on this page, buy a G15 840i with the B58 straight-six — it's the most dependable engine ever fitted to the 8 Series, delivers 340 bhp and 33 mpg, and the chassis is magnificent. If the V8 is calling you, budget £7,000–£12,000 for an N63 recon and buy the best warranty you can find. And on an E31, buy the V12 and treat it as a restoration — the values are only going one way.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW 8 Series Engine Replacement Costs — By Generation
replacementCosts.subHeadline: Real UK pricing for used, reconditioned and rebuilt engines — supply only, with labour guidance.
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Labour estimate: Fitting a BMW 8 Series engine typically takes 10–24 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW-QUOTE]. The 8 Series rule — grand tourer, grand bills: A £7,000–£12,000+ N63 recon against a £35,000 used G15 M850i sits at 20–34% of the car's value — ⚠️ watch. Against a £100,000 new G16, the same repair is just 7–12% — ✅ safe. Against a £70,000 M8, a £9,000–£14,000+ S63 recon is 13–20% — ✅ safe. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. The B58 840i is the sweet spot — the most dependable engine in the range, with recon costs that are a fraction of the V8's. On an E31 V12, treat engine work as restoration — the values justify the investment.

engineEvolution.h2: How the BMW 8 Series Engine Range Evolved — 1990 to Today

eras.whyBmwChanged: BMW's flagship GT launched with the M70 V12 — the first production V12 from BMW. The V8 arrived in 1993 (840Ci) as a more affordable option.
eras.worthKnowing: The M70 and M73 V12s are surprisingly durable — 200k+ miles is possible with proper maintenance. The S70 in the 850CSi is one of BMW's rarest and most valuable engines.

eras.whyBmwChanged: BMW discontinued the E31 in 1999 due to poor sales — the 6 Series (E63/E64) replaced it as the grand tourer. The 8 Series nameplate lay dormant for nearly two decades.
eras.worthKnowing: During this period, the E31 became a cult classic. Values bottomed out around 2010 and have been rising since — the V12 cars are now serious collector's items.

eras.whyBmwChanged: BMW revived the 8 Series in 2018 on the CLAR platform. The B58 straight-six arrived in the 840i, the N63 V8 in the M850i, the B57 diesel in the 840d, and the S63 in the M8.
eras.worthKnowing: The B58 is the most dependable engine ever fitted to an 8 Series — no V8 caveats, robust cooling, and proven reliability from the 3 Series and 5 Series. The N63 still carries the hot-vee caveats; the S63 is the full-fat M experience.

whoShouldBuy.h2: Who Should Buy a BMW 8 Series?

profiles.verdict: The 8 Series is built for this — the G15 840i with the B58 delivers 340 bhp, 33 mpg, and wafting comfort across European motorways. The B58 is bulletproof; the chassis is magnificent. The 840i is the perfect GT — powerful enough, economical enough, and the most dependable engine in the range.

The 8 Series is a grand tourer, not a commuter car — it's long, wide, and thirsty (especially the V8s). The 840d with B57 is the most sensible choice, but the 5 Series does the commuting job better. Buy an 840i if you must, but the 5 Series is the sensible choice.

One of the most dangerous badges on this site for a tight budget. A £30,000 used M850i is not a cheap 8 Series — it's an expensive one with the first year's bills prepaid by nobody. A £35,000 840i is the bargain; a £30,000 M850i without a warranty is the trap.

The M8 with the S63 is the full-fat experience — 600+ bhp, 3.2s to 60, and a V8 roar that justifies the bills. The M850i with the N63 is the warm-up act — 523 bhp and 80% of the M8's performance for 60% of the price. Buy the best warranty you can find.

The E31 V12 is a rising classic — the M70/M73 cars are the ones to buy, and values are only going one way. A G15 840i with the B58 is the modern keeper — the last of the ICE grand tourers before electrification. Both are worth proper investment.

calculatorCta.h2: Can I Repair My BMW 8 Series Engine — or Should I Replace It?
calculatorCta.intro: Rattling on cold start from an N63? Coolant loss from a B58? On the 8 Series, the answer swings on your car's value and which engine you have — a £35,000 used M850i and a £100,000 new G16 face very different economics. Get a personalised repair-vs-replace verdict using real UK cost data.
